title: Bytes, Encodings, and Cryptographic Primitive Selection · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed security review reconstructs bytes, encodings, and cryptographic primitive selection from threat, algorithm, parameter, implementation, key-custody, failure, test-vector, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for byte representation and primitive decision table after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Trace strings, UTF-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning.
Distinguish encoding, checksums, hashing, MACs, signatures, encryption, key agreement, KEMs, and password hashing by purpose and reversibility.
Choose a vetted high-level construction that supplies the required combined properties instead of composing low-level primitives casually.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Crypto Go Bytes Text Boundary · changed case · Review Crypto Go Bytes Text Boun…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 4665 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate encoding, hashing, authentication, confidentiality, reversibility, and canonical domain-bound bytes; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace strings, UTF-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning.

Build deterministic Go evidence to separate encoding, hashing, authentication, confidentiality, reversibility, and canonical domain-bound bytes; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define injective length-delimited serialization and reject ambiguous or non-canonical inputs before signing or authenticating.
